part_title: Section Twenty-Five: Final Recommendations by Responsible Stakeholder
part_subtitle: Stakeholder-Specific Recommendations for Sustainable Management of Tuta absoluta

items.text: Tuta absoluta cannot be managed sustainably through a single decision made within a tomato field because the pest life cycle extends across a broad production system that includes:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta cannot be managed sustainably through a single decision made within a tomato field because the pest life cycle extends across a broad production system that includes:

items.text: One field may implement an advanced programme and still fail because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]One field may implement an advanced programme and still fail because of:

items: Infested transplants arriving from the nursery.
A neighbouring old field that was not terminated.
Use of an unsuitable pesticide in a nearby field.
Movement of infested fruits and residues.
Poor quality of a commercial natural enemy.
Absence of a unified warning system.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]A neighbouring old field that was not terminated.
[[prepared]]Use of an unsuitable pesticide in a nearby field.
[[prepared]]Poor quality of a commercial natural enemy.

items.text: Distribution of responsibilities, data sharing, and holding each stakeholder accountable for the part of the pest cycle under its control.
The system should be based on the following principles:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The system should be based on the following principles:

items: 1. Every stakeholder is responsible for preventing the production or movement of a new pest source.
2. Responsibility for failure should not be placed entirely on the grower or the insecticide.
3. Movement of transplants, pesticides, produce, and residues should be documented.
4. Warnings should be shared among neighbouring fields.
5. Commercial interests should be separated from scientific product evaluation.
6. No biological or chemical product should be adopted without quality control.
7. The factory should be linked to the field programme before harvest, not only after rejecting the consignment.
8. Research organisations should provide solutions that are applicable and economically measurable.
9. Regulatory authorities should support area-wide management, not merely paper registration.
10. Every stakeholder is responsible for protecting the future efficacy of insecticides and natural enemies.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Every stakeholder is responsible for preventing the production or movement of a new pest source.
[[prepared]]2. Responsibility for failure should not be placed entirely on the grower or the insecticide.
[[prepared]]3. Movement of transplants, pesticides, produce, and residues should be documented.
[[prepared]]4. Warnings should be shared among neighbouring fields.
[[prepared]]5. Commercial interests should be separated from scientific product evaluation.
[[prepared]]6. No biological or chemical product should be adopted without quality control.
[[prepared]]7. The factory should be linked to the field programme before harvest, not only after rejecting the consignment.
[[prepared]]8. Research organisations should provide solutions that are applicable and economically measurable.
[[prepared]]9. Regulatory authorities should support area-wide management, not merely paper registration.
[[prepared]]10. Every stakeholder is responsible for protecting the future efficacy of insecticides and natural enemies.

items.text: Recommendations for the Field Agricultural Engineer
The agricultural engineer is the centre for integrating information, not merely the executor of a spray programme.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The agricultural engineer is the centre for integrating information, not merely the executor of a spray programme.

items.text: Engineer performance should not be measured by the number of treatment orders issued, but by the ability to protect the crop with the least possible disruption to the production system.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Engineer performance should not be measured by the number of treatment orders issued, but by the ability to protect the crop with the least possible disruption to the production system.

items: 1. Ensure that traps are installed before or at the beginning of transplanting.
2. Separate edge traps from interior traps.
4. Inspect eggs, mines, larvae, and fruits.
5. Distinguish active mines from old mines.
10. Avoid making a decision from a single trap reading.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]1. Ensure that traps are installed before or at the beginning of transplanting.
[[prepared]]2. Separate edge traps from interior traps.
[[prepared]]5. Distinguish active mines from old mines.
[[prepared]]10. Avoid making a decision from a single trap reading.

items.text: Should not be issued without identifying the stage, sector, and objective.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Should not be issued without identifying the stage, sector, and objective.

items: Avoid repeating a group under a different trade name.
Collect samples when resistance is suspected.
Introduce periods relying on non-chemical measures.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoid repeating a group under a different trade name.

items: Review the effect of every pesticide on them.
Select timings that reduce exposure of beneficial organisms.
Monitor recovery of natural enemies after intervention.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Review the effect of every pesticide on them.
[[prepared]]Select timings that reduce exposure of beneficial organisms.
[[prepared]]Monitor recovery of natural enemies after intervention.

items.text: Beneficial organisms should not be treated as a marginal note in the report.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Beneficial organisms should not be treated as a marginal note in the report.

items.text: The engineer should refuse to issue a treatment order when there is no:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The engineer should refuse to issue a treatment order when there is no:

items: Sufficient time for the method to act.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Sufficient time for the method to act.

items.text: The engineer may be evaluated according to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The engineer may be evaluated according to:

items: Detection of infestation before it reaches fruits.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Detection of infestation before it reaches fruits.

items.text: The grower is a partner in decision-making, not a passive recipient of recommendations.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The grower is a partner in decision-making, not a passive recipient of recommendations.

items: Complying with the rate and preharvest interval.
Informing neighbours when severe infestation occurs.
Terminating the field when it is no longer economically viable.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Complying with the rate and preharvest interval.
[[prepared]]Terminating the field when it is no longer economically viable.

items: Request a treatment record for the batch.
Reject trays carrying mines or widespread infestation.
Avoid mixing transplant sources without documentation.
Retain the nursery name and batch number.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Request a treatment record for the batch.
[[prepared]]Reject trays carrying mines or widespread infestation.
[[prepared]]Retain the nursery name and batch number.

items.text: Infested transplants should not be accepted merely because they are cheaper.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Infested transplants should not be accepted merely because they are cheaper.

items.text: The grower should not demand spraying merely because of:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The grower should not demand spraying merely because of:

items: A high reading in one trap.
Observing a neighbouring field being sprayed.

items.text: Likewise, necessary intervention should not be delayed when there is confirmed:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Likewise, necessary intervention should not be delayed when there is confirmed:

items: Avoid purchasing an insecticide by trade name alone.
Avoid mixing several products without review.
Avoid repeating a material that has shown reduced efficacy.
Avoid spraying the biological belt without reason.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Avoid purchasing an insecticide by trade name alone.
[[prepared]]Avoid repeating a material that has shown reduced efficacy.
[[prepared]]Avoid spraying the biological belt without reason.

items: Avoid moving them into another field.
Remove the crop when economic viability ends.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Remove the crop when economic viability ends.

items.text: The grower should notify neighbouring fields when:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The grower should notify neighbouring fields when:

items: Removal of a heavily infested field begins.
Infested transplants from a shared source are discovered.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Removal of a heavily infested field begins.
[[prepared]]Infested transplants from a shared source are discovered.

items.text: Concealing infestation may increase pressure across the entire area.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Concealing infestation may increase pressure across the entire area.

items.text: Recommendations for the Farm or Project Manager
The farm manager converts the technical programme into an implementable system through:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The farm manager converts the technical programme into an implementable system through:

items.text: A substitute should be available for each role in case of absence.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A substitute should be available for each role in case of absence.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A serious management error is to reduce inspection costs and then spend several times more on corrective treatments.

items.text: The treatment order should come from one authorised source after data review.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The treatment order should come from one authorised source after data review.

items: Issue an independent decision for each sector.
Avoid spraying the entire project because of localised infestation.

items.text: Treating the Nursery as the First Line of Defence
A nursery distributing infested transplants does not merely transfer a few larvae; it may establish tens or hundreds of hotspots across multiple fields.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]A nursery distributing infested transplants does not merely transfer a few larvae; it may establish tens or hundreds of hotspots across multiple fields.

items.text: Freedom of transplants from the pest should therefore be considered a basic quality criterion.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Freedom of transplants from the pest should therefore be considered a basic quality criterion.

items: Prevent entry of infested tomato fruits or plants.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Prevent entry of infested tomato fruits or plants.

items.text: Inspection frequency should increase when regional activity rises.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Inspection frequency should increase when regional activity rises.

items.text: This enables notification of all clients when infestation linked to the batch is detected.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]This enables notification of all clients when infestation linked to the batch is detected.

items: 6. Implement a documented intervention where necessary.
8. Inform customers who received transplants from the same batch.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]6. Implement a documented intervention where necessary.

items.text: The case should not be concealed to protect commercial reputation.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The case should not be concealed to protect commercial reputation.

items.text: Recommendations for Pesticide Companies and Distributors
The company's responsibility is not limited to demonstrating toxicity; it also includes protecting product efficacy and reducing misuse.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The company's responsibility is not limited to demonstrating toxicity; it also includes protecting product efficacy and reducing misuse.

items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Statements such as the following should be prohibited or avoided:

Completely safe for all beneficial organisms.

items.text: They should be replaced with specific claims supported by registration and studies.

items.text: The distributor should be trained to:

items: Avoid selling two materials from the same group as rotation.
Reject suggestions to increase the rate.
Refer failure cases for technical investigation.

items.text: Reports should not be rejected automatically as misuse, nor accepted automatically as resistance.

The Factory as a Partner in Field Management
The factory's role should not be limited to accepting or rejecting produce at the delivery gate.

A mechanism for field removal after delivery.

This allows the true loss associated with the pest to be determined.

items.text: Moving from Field Control to System Management
Supporting pesticide purchase alone is insufficient.
The state or production sectors need:
Legislation Related to Residues and Waste
Regulations should preferably require large projects, nurseries, and grading stations to:
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Regulations should preferably require large projects, nurseries, and grading stations to:

items.text: Indicators of Success of the Shared System

items: Increased proportion of fields using traps and plant inspection.
Increased documented use of natural enemies.
Shorter interval between final harvest and field removal.
Greater grower participation in warning systems.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Increased proportion of fields using traps and plant inspection.
[[prepared]]Shorter interval between final harvest and field removal.

items: Continued distribution of transplants from unknown sources.
Failure to share cases of reduced efficacy.
Leaving factory and field residues unmanaged.
Repeated rejection of consignments because of residues.
Lack of coordination among production cycles.
prepared_items: [[prepared]]Continued distribution of transplants from unknown sources.
[[prepared]]Failure to share cases of reduced efficacy.
[[prepared]]Repeated rejection of consignments because of residues.

items.text: Tuta absoluta is not merely an insecticide problem, an individual grower problem, or a one-season problem.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tuta absoluta is not merely an insecticide problem, an individual grower problem, or a one-season problem.

items.text: It becomes a production-system problem when:

items: Pesticides are used without resistance management.

items.text: It becomes manageable when every stakeholder assumes responsibility within a unified system beginning in the nursery and ending only after field removal.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]It becomes manageable when every stakeholder assumes responsibility within a unified system beginning in the nursery and ending only after field removal.

items.text: Sustainable management of Tuta absoluta depends on clear allocation of responsibilities across the entire tomato-production chain.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Sustainable management of Tuta absoluta depends on clear allocation of responsibilities across the entire tomato-production chain.

items.text: The agricultural engineer is responsible for integrating data from traps, eggs, larvae, fruits, and natural enemies into a documented decision, rather than issuing a spray schedule.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The agricultural engineer is responsible for integrating data from traps, eggs, larvae, fruits, and natural enemies into a documented decision, rather than issuing a spray schedule.

items.text: The grower is responsible for using healthy transplants, complying with rates and preharvest intervals, collecting infested fruits, and terminating the field.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The grower is responsible for using healthy transplants, complying with rates and preharvest intervals, collecting infested fruits, and terminating the field.

items.text: The farm manager is responsible for providing resources, organising the team, preventing conflicting decisions, and reviewing economic performance.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The farm manager is responsible for providing resources, organising the team, preventing conflicting decisions, and reviewing economic performance.

items.text: The nursery represents the first line of defence and should implement inspection, traceability, isolation, and reporting systems for infested batches.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]The nursery represents the first line of defence and should implement inspection, traceability, isolation, and reporting systems for infested batches.

items.text: Producers of natural enemies are responsible for the quality of species, strain, number, emergence percentage, transport, and technical support, while avoiding transfer of greenhouse release rates to open fields without calibration.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Producers of natural enemies are responsible for the quality of species, strain, number, emergence percentage, transport, and technical support, while avoiding transfer of greenhouse release rates to open fields without calibration.

items.text: Pesticide companies are responsible for providing correct information on the active ingredient, IRAC group, target stage, and preharvest interval, and for conducting scientific investigation of reduced-efficacy cases.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Pesticide companies are responsible for providing correct information on the active ingredient, IRAC group, target stage, and preharvest interval, and for conducting scientific investigation of reduced-efficacy cases.

items.text: Tomato-processing factories should participate in management from the beginning of the season through technical contracts, accepted-material lists, monitoring, and support for field removal, while properly managing receiving and processing waste.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Tomato-processing factories should participate in management from the beginning of the season through technical contracts, accepted-material lists, monitoring, and support for field removal, while properly managing receiving and processing waste.

items.text: Grading stations and export companies are responsible for traceability, prevention of infested-fruit accumulation, and review of residues and market specifications.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Grading stations and export companies are responsible for traceability, prevention of infested-fruit accumulation, and review of residues and market specifications.

items.text: Researchers should develop multi-site, multi-season Egyptian evidence that measures fruit infestation, accepted yield, and economic viability rather than insect mortality alone.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Researchers should develop multi-site, multi-season Egyptian evidence that measures fruit infestation, accepted yield, and economic viability rather than insect mortality alone.

items.text: Extension services should convert research findings into practical instructions, images, and warning templates.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Extension services should convert research findings into practical instructions, images, and warning templates.

items.text: Regulatory authorities need to monitor pesticide, natural-enemy, and nursery quality, establish a resistance-monitoring network, and update information available to engineers.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]Regulatory authorities need to monitor pesticide, natural-enemy, and nursery quality, establish a resistance-monitoring network, and update information available to engineers.

items.text: No single stakeholder can eliminate the Tuta absoluta problem. Integration among stakeholders can, however, shift management from a late spray-based reaction to a preventive system that anticipates infestation and protects the crop, insecticide efficacy, and natural enemies.
items.prepared_text: [[prepared]]No single stakeholder can eliminate the Tuta absoluta problem. Integration among stakeholders can, however, shift management from a late spray-based reaction to a preventive system that anticipates infestation and protects the crop, insecticide efficacy, and natural enemies.

Biondi, A., Guedes, R. N. C., Wan, F. H., & Desneux, N. (2018). Ecology, worldwide spread, and management of the invasive South American tomato pinworm, Tuta absoluta. Annual Review of Entomology, 63, 239–258.

Guedes, R. N. C., Roditakis, E., Campos, M. R., et al. (2019). Insecticide resistance in the tomato pinworm Tuta absoluta: Patterns, spread, mechanisms, management and outlook. Journal of Pest Science, 92, 1329–1342.
Ferracini, C., Bueno, V. H. P., Dindo, M. L., et al. (2019). Natural enemies of Tuta absoluta in the Mediterranean basin, Europe and South America. Biocontrol Science and Technology, 29, 578–609.
Colmenárez, Y. C., Corniani, N., Jahnke, S. M., Sampaio, M. V., & Vásquez, G. M. (2022). Sustainable management of the invasive Tuta absoluta. Journal of Integrated Pest Management, 13, 15.

Hassan, E. S. M., Mesbah, I. I., Ali, F. A., & El-Shesheny, I. A. (2022). Prevalence, population dynamics and associated natural enemies of tomato leafminer, Tuta absoluta, in Egypt. International Journal of Tropical Insect Science, 42, 143–162.
Kansiime, K. M., Karanja, P., Rware, H., et al. (2020). Integrated Management of Tomato Leaf Miner and Other Tomato Pests. CABI and Koppert Biological Systems.
Landis, D. A., Wratten, S. D., & Gurr, G. M. (2000). Habitat management to conserve natural enemies of arthropod pests in agriculture. Annual Review of Entomology, 45, 175–201.
